title: 2022 Salford Red Devils season
wikitext:
{{Short description|Salford Red Devils season}} {{Infobox rugby league team season | year = 2022 | team_colour = red; border: solid black 2px | font_colour = white; | league = Super League | league_link = [[Super League XXVII|Super League]] | league_rank = 6 | league_wins = 14 | league_draws = 0 | league_losses = 13 | points_for = 700 | points_against = 622 | playoff_result = Semi-finals | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = Sixth round | cup_wins = 0 | cup_losses = 1 | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = [[Ian Blease]] | coach_title = Head Coach | coach = [[Paul Rowley]] | captain = [[Elijah Taylor (rugby league)|Elijah Taylor]] | stadium = [[AJ Bell Stadium]]
[[Barton-upon-Irwell]], [[Greater Manchester]] | avg_attendance = 4,690 | agg_attendance = 58,888 | high_attendance = 6,041 | low_attendance = 3,221 | top_tries = [[Ken Sio]] (28) | top_goals = [[Marc Sneyd]] (105) | top_points = Marc Sneyd (228) | prev_season = [[2021 Salford Red Devils season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 Salford Red Devils season|2023]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Salford Red Devils seasons }} In the 2022 rugby league season, [[Salford Red Devils]] competed in [[Super League XXVII]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. Salford played at the [[AJ Bell Stadium]] and were coached by [[Paul Rowley]]. ==Season review== On 11 February, Salford began their Super League campaign with a 26–16 away win at [[Castleford Tigers]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} which they followed with a 38–12 win over [[Toulouse Olympique]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} After this Salford began a poor run of results which saw them knocked out in the sixth round of the [[Challenge Cup]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36891/betfred-challenge-cup-round-6-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round 6 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=27 March 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} and fall to tenth in the table by early May.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60019/betfred-super-league-round-12-preview |title=Betfred Super League Round 12 Preview |publisher=RFL |date=12 May 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} However, their season then began to improve with back-to-back wins against [[Leeds Rhinos]] and Castleford{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60354/betfred-super-league-preview-%7C-round-16 |title=Betfred Super League Preview {{!}} Round 16 |publisher=RFL |date=23 June 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} and although a win over [[Wakefield Trinity]] in June was seen as boosting their chances of survival,{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60378/betfred-super-league-round-16-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 16 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=26 June 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} by mid-July their win over [[Catalans Dragons]] was described in terms of them possibly reaching the play-offs.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60519/betfred-super-league-round-19-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 19 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=26 June 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} A 44–12 upset of league leaders' [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60644/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-21|title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 21 |publisher=RFL |date=1 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} was part of a strong late season that also included wins over [[Huddersfield Giants]] and Castleford{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60738/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-23 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 23 |publisher=RFL |date=15 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60825/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}}publisher=RFL |date=30 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} and meant that Salford went into the final round of the regular season having already qualified for the play-offs and so named six debutants in the squad for their 32–14 loss to the [[Warrington Wolves]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} After finishing sixth in the table, Salford faced third-placed Huddersfield in the play-offs and defeated them 28–0 to set up a semi-final fixture with St Helens.{{cite web|url= https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60894/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-september-10 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} September 10 |publisher=RFL |date=10 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} On 17 September, their season was ended at the [[Totally Wicked Stadium]] by a 19–12 loss to the defending champions.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60938/leeds-and-st-helens-to-meet-in-betfred-super-league-grand-final |title=Leeds and St Helens to meet in Betfred Super League Grand Final |publisher=RFL |date=18 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} At the 2022 Super League Awards on 20 September, [[Brodie Croft]] was named the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60957/brodie-croft-named-steve-prescott-mbe-man-of-steel-for-2022 |title=Brodie Croft named Steve Prescott MBE Man of Steel for 2022 |publisher=RFL |date=21 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} ==Results== {{clear}} ===Pre-season friendlies=== {{legend2|#CFC|Win|border=1px solid #000000|text=W}} {{legend2|#FFE3E3|Loss|border=1px solid
